title: Grimoire blueprint (v1)
description: Compilable blueprint format consumed by the Forge (grimoire serve) and by `grimoire ext publish` / `grimoire ext add-blueprint`. A v1 blueprint declares typed nodes with explicit pins; edges connect pins whose contracts must match. Studio drafts (blueprintVersion 2, or nodes without a `pins` key) are NOT covered by this schema: they are converted to v1 by the Forge before lint/simulate/compile.

blueprintVersion.description: Format version. Must be the integer 1: `grimoire ext publish` refuses any other value, and nodes carrying a `pins` key mark the file as v1 (not a Studio draft).

id.description: Blueprint identifier: lowercase letters and digits separated by single hyphens (e.g. `web-pipeline`). Also used as the file stem (`<id>.blueprint.json`) and the compiled mission pack name (`.github/prompts/<id>.blueprint.prompt.md`).

name.description: Human-readable display name. Optional; defaults to the id in listings and in the compiled mission pack.

description.description: Free-text description. Optional; copied verbatim into the compiled mission pack.

catalogRef.description: Pin of the pattern catalogue the blueprint was authored against. The compiler traces `catalogRef.version` into the generated artifact for drift detection.

version.description: Catalogue version (e.g. `1.0.0`). See `catalogVersion` in the catalogue export.

nodes.description: Flow nodes. Must be non-empty. Every node MUST carry a `pins` key (even empty): if all nodes lack it, the file is treated as a Studio draft and re-projected, bypassing the declared typing.

edges.description: Typed connections between pins. Required (may be empty for a single-node flow). Each endpoint is `<nodeId>.<pinId>` and both pins must declare the same contract.

extensions.description: Extensions the blueprint depends on. `grimoire ext add-blueprint` compares these ids against the installed extensions and reports the missing ones (with the install command to run).

id.description: Extension id as published in the registry (e.g. `crewai`).

compiled.description: Written by the compiler after a successful `blueprint_compile`: timestamp, catalogue version and hashes of the generated artifacts. Do not author this section by hand.

path.description: Project-relative path of the generated artifact.

sourceNode.description: Blueprint id (or node id) the artifact was generated from.

meta.description: Editor metadata (validated/simulated flags, timestamps). Ignored by the compiler.

identifier.description: Node and pin identifiers must not contain dots or whitespace: edge endpoints are parsed by splitting `<nodeId>.<pinId>` on the first dot.

node.description: A flow node. `kind` selects how `ref` is resolved and which prerequisites the simulation checks.

id.description: Node id, unique within the blueprint (duplicate ids are a blocking error).

kind.description: Node kind. `pattern`: catalogue pattern (ref = pattern id). `artifact`: governed project file (ref = project-relative path, must exist at validation time). `extension-node`: node provided by an extension (ref = `<extId>/<nodeId>`, the extension must be installed to compile). `composite`: sub-flow (ref = `use-case:<id>` or a `.blueprint.json` path). `composite-inline`: flattened Studio group (informational). `agent-spec`: agent/trigger sketch from the Studio (informational).

ref.description: Target reference; shape depends on `kind` (see the per-kind constraints below).

label.description: Display label. Optional; used in listings, simulation steps and the compiled mission pack.

description.description: Free-text note about the node's role in the flow. Optional, informational.

pins.description: Typed connection points. REQUIRED on every v1 node (an empty list is fine for an unconnected node): a missing `pins` key on all nodes downgrades the file to a Studio draft.

ref.description: Catalogue pattern id, e.g. `ORC-01`, `QUA-04` (checked against the catalogue when one is available).
`<extensionId>/<nodeId>` as declared in the extension manifest `provides.nodes` (e.g. `crewai/crewai-crew`). Compilation is blocked until the extension is installed in the project.
Either `use-case:<catalogue-use-case-id>` or a project-relative path to a sub-blueprint ending in `.blueprint.json`.

pin.description: Typed connection point of a node. An edge endpoint `<nodeId>.<pinId>` resolves to a pin, and the contracts on both ends must be identical.

id.description: Pin id, unique within its node (e.g. `in`, `out`, `mission`, `result`).

direction.description: `in`: the pin receives; `out`: the pin emits.

contract.description: Contract carried by the pin (e.g. `task-envelope`, `handoff-packet`, `evidence-pack`). Checked against the catalogue contracts when one is available.

edge.description: Connection between two pins. Both endpoints must exist and carry the same contract; a declared `contract` must match the pins' contract. Edges also define execution order (the flow must be acyclic to compile).

from.description: Source endpoint `<nodeId>.<pinId>`; the pin should have direction `out`.

to.description: Destination endpoint `<nodeId>.<pinId>`; the pin should have direction `in`.

contract.description: Optional explicit contract. When present it must equal the contract of both connected pins (mismatch is a blocking error).

channel.description: Execution channel of the edge (P0.2). `happy` (default) is the nominal path; `failure` carries retry/fallback/compensation/dead-letter routing; `escalation` routes to human or higher-authority handling. Absence equals `happy` — existing blueprints migrate without loss.
